criteria.name: Interface completeness
criteria.evidence: Exports CacheConfig (line 15), CacheStats (line 21), Interface (line 29) with getOrCompute, invalidate, invalidateKey, stats methods. All signatures include required parameters.

criteria.name: TTL defaults
criteria.evidence: getOrCompute accepts optional CacheConfig. Defaults: maxEntries=1000 (line 103), ttl=Duration.minutes(5) (line 101), maxEntrySize=1024*1024 (line 104). All defaults correctly applied via ?? operator.

criteria.name: Clock
criteria.evidence: Uses `Clock.currentTimeMillis` from Effect (line 100). No `Date.now()` usage anywhere in the file.

criteria.name: LRU promotion and eviction
criteria.evidence: Hit promotion uses map.delete(key)+map.set(key,entry) re-insertion which pushes to MRU end via Map insertion ordering (lines 110-111). LRU eviction during insert iterates map keys (first = oldest) and deletes while over maxEntries (lines 145-152). Map preserves insertion order so .keys().next() returns the LRU entry.

criteria.name: Stats completeness
criteria.evidence: stats() returns { size, hits, misses, evictions, estimatedBytes } (lines 185-197). All counters backed by Refs: hits (line 51), misses (line 52), evictions (line 53), estimatedBytes (line 54).

criteria.name: Expiry checking and removal
criteria.evidence: Expired entries checked on access: `if (entry.expiresAt > t)` for hit (line 109), else expired removal (line 114). Expired sweep also performed during new insertion loop (lines 137-141).

criteria.name: Max entry size skipping
criteria.evidence: `approximateSize` (lines 42-48) serializes to JSON and measures bytes. Entry is skipped (not cached) if size > maxEntrySize (line 130). Deferred is still succeeded to unblock awaiters (line 164). Span annotation on skip.

criteria.name: Span annotations
criteria.evidence: Annotated spans for: hit (line 112), miss with dedup (line 122), miss without dedup (line 126), expired eviction (lines 115-117), LRU eviction (lines 153-155), maxEntrySize skip (lines 164-167). All use `Effect.annotateCurrentSpan`.

criteria.name: Inflight dedup preservation
criteria.details: MAJOR finding below — defect propagation gap
criteria.evidence: Inflight dedup structure preserved: Map<CacheKey, Deferred.Deferred<any>> in Ref (line 49). Check before compute (lines 119-123), create+store new Deferred (lines 127-128), cleanup in Effect.ensuring (lines 131-135).

criteria.name: Backward compatibility of invalidate
criteria.evidence: invalidate() creates new Map (Ref.set(cache, new Map())) and resets estimatedBytes to 0 (lines 169-171). invalidateKey() removes the specific key and recalculates bytes (lines 173-182). Both preserve the clear/remove contract.

findings.description: When `compute()` defects (uncaught exception, case: 'Die'), the inflight dedup Deferred `d` is never completed. `Effect.ensuring` properly cleans up the inflight map entry, but the Deferred itself remains unresolved. Concurrent callers who entered via the inflight dedup path and are awaiting `Deferred.await(existing)` (line 123) will hang forever because their Deferred is never succeeded or failed.
findings.reproduction:
1. Request A calls getOrCompute('X', ...) — no cache, no inflight entry → creates Deferred d, starts compute
2. Request B calls getOrCompute('X', ...) — finds d in inflight → awaits d
3. A's compute defects (e.g., throws synchronously inside compute)
4. Effect.ensuring cleanup runs, removes key from inflight map
5. Deferred d is never resolved → B hangs forever
findings.evidence: Deferred is only succeeded (never failed/died) on lines 157 and 162 via `Deferred.succeed`. No `Deferred.fail`, `Deferred.die`, or `Deferred.done` call exists. The Effect library provides `Deferred.into` (Deferred.ts:1226) which correctly handles this by capturing `Effect.exit(self)` and completing the Deferred with the full Exit, including defects.
findings.repair: Replace the manual Deferred.succeed approach with `Deferred.into(compute(), d)` or use `Effect.exit(compute())` + `Deferred.done(d, exit)` to ensure defects are propagated. Also import `Exit` from 'effect'.

findings.description: Cache hit promotion (lines 110-111), expired entry removal (line 114), and inflight entry insertion (line 127) mutate Map objects directly after obtaining them via `Ref.get()`. These mutations modify the Ref's internal state through the shared mutable reference, but bypass the `Ref.update` pattern used consistently elsewhere (LRU eviction on lines 136-153, invalidate on line 169, invalidateKey on line 176, inflight cleanup on line 132).
findings.evidence:
Line 108: `const map = yield* Ref.get(cache)` — obtains Map reference
Lines 110-111: `map.delete(key); map.set(key, entry)` — mutates Ref's Map directly
Line 114: `map.delete(key)` — mutates Ref's Map directly
Line 119: `const pending = yield* Ref.get(inflight)` — obtains Map reference
Line 127: `pending.set(key, d)` — mutates Ref's Map directly
Compare with line 136: `yield* Ref.update(cache, (m) => { ... m.set(key, ...); return m })` — correct pattern
findings.repair: Replace direct mutations with `Ref.update` calls: wrap hit promotion and expired entry removal inside `Ref.update(cache, ...)` blocks, and inflight insertion inside `Ref.update(inflight, ...)`.

findings.description: The inflight dedup check-and-set is non-atomic: `Ref.get(inflight)` returns the map, then `pending.get(key)` checks it, then `pending.set(key, d)` adds the new Deferred. Between the check and the set, another concurrent request can also find no existing entry and create its own Deferred. Result: two concurrent computations for the same key.
findings.evidence: Lines 119-127: check and set are separate Ref.get + direct mutation operations with no atomic boundary.
findings.repair: Use `Ref.modify` for an atomic check-and-set: `yield* Ref.modify(inflight, (m) => { const existing = m.get(key); if (existing) return [existing, m]; const d = Deferred.makeUnsafe(); m.set(key, d); return [d, m]; })`

recommendation: Address the MAJOR finding (inflight Deferred defect propagation) before production use. The MINOR consistency finding is advisory. The cache is otherwise well-structured and all 10 criteria pass.
